page: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dpsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by an ARM64 Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU architecture lacks hyperthreading, providing exactly one thread per core. Memory capacity is 16.0 GB, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and a large 128 MB L3 cache. It supports AES cryptography but lacks AVX, AMX, and other x86-specific instruction sets. There is no bundled local storage or GPU.

Performance benchmarks place the D4ps_v6 in the average range for most general-purpose workloads. It demonstrates strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 32.94 GB/sec and a strong PassMark memory score. However, its cryptographic performance is poor, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 10%. Ray tracing and image processing benchmarks are weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is solid, reaching 454.62 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 88.86 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

Utilizing ARM-based Neoverse-N2 processors, the D4ps_v6 offers a power-efficient and cost-effective profile for general-purpose workloads that do not require x86-specific vector extensions. It is well-suited for static web serving, database operations, and software compilation. It is less optimal for heavy cryptographic tasks, image processing, or ray tracing due to instruction set limitations and lower benchmark scores in those areas.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v6 is an ARM-based general-purpose virtual machine powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks show average CPU and multi-threaded decompression capabilities, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression and memory performance. However, it exhibits weak performance in ray tracing, image processing, and cryptographic encryption due to the absence of x86-specific vector extensions. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations where ARM architecture efficiency provides a qualitative cost-to-performance advantage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v6 is an ARM64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Neoverse-N2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong memory performance, optimized for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, optimized for general-purpose and web serving workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose virtual machine offering balanced compute and memory resources for efficient web serving and database operations.
bullet_points: ARM64 architecture featuring 4 dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 physical cores at 3.4 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and memory operations
Lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing remote Premium Storage
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
